speaker: speaker1
text: Are there any interesting stories in the newspaper today ?

speaker: speaker2
text: There are a few stories about natural disasters . There is a massive forest fire in Australia . It covers several square kilometers of land has destroyed many more square kilometers .

speaker: speaker1
text: Was it caused accidentally or naturally ? Sometimes it gets so hot that fires begin naturally in Australia and Africa .

speaker: speaker2
text: Nobody knows at the moment , but it has been very hot there recently . The drought in Africa is causing starvation . Millions of people have migrated to try and find food .

speaker: speaker1
text: What is the international community doing to help ?

speaker: speaker2
text: The European union has sent several plants with relief supplies . Several countries have sent soldiers to distribute food and medical supplies . Refugee camps have been set up across the region .

speaker: speaker1
text: How many countries have been affected ?

speaker: speaker2
text: Six have been seriously affected , but the refugees are also migrating to several surrounding countries .

speaker: speaker1
text: I saw on the tv yesterday that there has been another earthquake in iran .

speaker: speaker2
text: Yes . There have been a few there recently . They say that this one was not a big quake . The Iranians are dealing with it on their own . They have purchased some special equipment to find people buried u

speaker: speaker1
text: Does the newspaper say anything about casualties ?

speaker: speaker2
text: So far , less than 20 people have died , but over 100 are in hospital .
